prose:
Method provenance – condensed Fukui functions (f+, f−, f0) for benzaldehyde
Property: Condensed Fukui functions per atom (electrophilic attack f+, nucleophilic attack f−, radical attack f0) and dual descriptor; identification of most electrophilic/nucleophilic sites.
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier “standard” in chemkit; no density-fitting – exact four-center integrals) via PySCF 2.* backend.
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates file “benzaldehyde.xyz”; no further optimisation – single-point population analyses only (DFT//input-xyz).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent requested).
Charge & multiplicity: charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet). N±1 states were computed as doublets (cation charge +1, mult 2; anion charge −1, mult 2).
Convergence & warnings: SCF cycles for the neutral, cation, and anion all converged; chemkit’s integrity checks report status "ok" and trustworthy = true, with three warnings reproduced verbatim below. No fall-backs or non-convergence occurred.
Live log / artifact paths: calculation was run through chemkit skill “fukui-reactivity”; live engine output was streamed to its .out file (path announced at run start) and the final JSON written to /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp052d3xk1/live.json. A bar-chart PNG of the atom-wise indices is at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/argo_o3/20260706-174036_benzaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png.
